title: transport.rs recv_text_frame wraps loop in tokio::time::timeout — timeout error message drops context
description: recv_text_frame uses tokio::time::timeout(..., async { loop { match ... }}).await.map_err(|_| anyhow::anyhow!('timeout waiting for relay response ({context})')). The timeout wraps the entire loop including the skipping of ping/pong frames. If a relay sends 999 pings before the challenge, the 30-second timeout counts all of them — the timeout is the right behavior. But the error message says 'timeout waiting for relay response' when context='challenge', which is slightly imprecise (it is waiting for any text frame, not a response). The parameter name 'context' suggests this is a descriptive string — but 'challenge' and 'auth response' are not self-describing to a user. A user who hits this timeout gets 'timeout waiting for relay response (auth response)' — the repetition of 'response' in the message is confusing. Suggested: 'timed out waiting 30s for {context} from relay'.
